report:
Workspace root: /Users/me/var/agent-workspaces/hZSNRqJizZeiegGSPCCh; remote origin git@github.com:juequinterore/aconcagua.git (issue_json carries no git.repository, so no mismatch check applies; the repo name 'aconcagua' is consistent with the issue subject).
Project: Astro 5.17.3 static marketing site (TypeScript strict, plain CSS w/ design tokens, no test suite, no linter), deployed to Firebase Hosting; locales es/en/zh with prefixDefaultLocale: false.
Validation gate: npm run build (also runs @astrojs/check). No tests exist.
Docs action: used existing docs/PROJECT.md (already comprehensive — no new docs/PROJECT.md created).
Spec written to specs/feat-1100-issue-100-link-to-blog-aconcagua-co.md.
Exposure surfaces wired up: src/components/Nav.astro navLinks (desktop list + mobile overlay both iterate the same array) and src/components/Footer.astro navLinks (footer 'Navegación' column). Because every page renders these two components, no per-page edits are required.
Affected files: src/components/Nav.astro (Modify navLinks + both render branches), src/components/Footer.astro (Modify navLinks + footer column render branch), src/i18n/{es,en,zh}.ts (add nav.blog + a11y.opens_new_tab).
Change tier: S — 5 file modifications, no new files, no schema, no new deps, no new routes.
Assumptions remaining: (a) opening blog.aconcagua.co in a new tab is preferred — chosen because the blog is on a different subdomain and the issue asks for 'not intrusive' (keeping the user on the marketing site); (b) the visible label remains the literal English/Spanish/Chinese word 'Blog' / 'Blog' / '博客' across locales — this is the natural choice and matches how 'WhatsApp', 'YouTube', 'Calendly' are used untranslated in the existing dictionaries.
